Cream Craver, Pune.

A brand that is not just visited, but remembered.
Outside the outlet, there is a huge pile of Alphonso mangoes.

Inside, the tables are full.

And in the customer’s mind, the association is very clear:

Cream Craver = Mango Cream.

That is the power of food branding.
Mangoes are seasonal.
But Cream Craver has turned mango season into a brand memory.

The display is simple, but powerful.
The product is familiar, but iconic.
The recall is not forced, it is built over years.

For food business owners, this is a very important lesson:

Your signature product should not just be another item on the menu.

It should become the reason people remember you, recommend you, and come back to you.

Branding is not only about logos, interiors, or packaging.

Real branding happens when customers can complete this sentence:

“If you go there, you must try their ______.”

That blank is where a strong food brand is built.

Hi, I’m Utkarsh Gulati, Founder of Upscale Lab.
At Upscale Lab, we help food businesses build stronger systems, sharper positioning, and better profitability.

What is the one product your brand should be known for?

One of the best dining experiences I’ve had in Pune! 🍽️✨

Visited Horn OK Please and the food was absolutely delicious. We tried Paneer Angara with roti — full of flavor and perfectly cooked. No compromise on taste at all! 😍

The place also has a beautiful Punjabi-style ambience that really adds to the whole experience. 🎶✨

Honestly, I was so busy enjoying the food that I completely forgot to click pictures of it 🤦‍♂️📸… but trust me, it was worth it! Definitely a place that won’t disappoint.
